findings.description: Redundant and potentially incorrect state updates in useCoordinator.ts. The 'transition' SSE listener calls applyTransition (which invokes setIssues) inside another setIssues functional update, which is an anti-pattern and causes redundant renders.
The POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick endpoint fails in Docker/Cloud environments because it depends on skills/worktree/scripts/worktree.py, which is not copied into the agent-coordinator Docker image.

Missing change_id in SyncPointStatus and ActiveWorktree contracts. The kick action requires change_id to uniquely identify registry entries, but it is not surfaced by the read endpoints, preventing the UI from providing the correct identifier.
SyncPointBanner.tsx passes 'unknown' as change_id to the kick endpoint. This will cause the backend's worktree teardown command to fail to locate and clear the stale agent entry from the registry.
skills/tests/agent-coordinator/test_kanban_viz_endpoints.py is dead code and a misleading duplicate of the active test file in agent-coordinator/tests/.
Ambiguous identifier mapping in get_sync_points_status. The agent_id field conflates agent_id and change_id from the registry, which can cause identification failures when they differ.
